---
title: "Theorem 3.2 Рђћ OLS normal equations and orthogonality of regressors and residuals"
lean_name: "HansenEconometrics.normal_equations"
chapter: 3
---

## Textbook statement

Hansen Theorem 3.2 states: when $X^\top X$ is invertible, the OLS estimator
$$\hat\beta = (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y$$
satisfies the **normal equations**
$$X^\top (y - X\hat\beta) = 0,$$
equivalently $X^\top \hat e = 0$ where $\hat e = y - X\hat\beta$ is the residual
vector. So the regressors are orthogonal to the OLS residuals.

## Assumptions

- Design matrix $X \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times k}$.
- $X^\top X$ is invertible (equivalently, $X$ has full column rank, $k \le n$).
- $y \in \mathbb{R}^n$ is the response vector. No distributional assumption is
  needed Рђћ Theorem 3.2 is purely linear-algebraic.

## Lean statement

The corresponding Lean objects all live in
`HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean`:

- [`olsBeta`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L20)
  (definition, line 20) Рђћ the closed-form OLS coefficient
  $(X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y$, defined under `[Invertible (Xрхђ * X)]`.
- [`fitted`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L38)
  (definition, line 38) Рђћ the fitted vector $X\hat\beta$.
- [`residual`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L42)
  (definition, line 42) Рђћ the residual vector $y - X\hat\beta$.
- [`normal_equations`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L46)
  (theorem, **line 46**) Рђћ the headline result:
  `Xрхђ *рхЦ residual X y = 0`.
- [`regressors_orthogonal_to_residual`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L61)
  (theorem, line 61) Рђћ same statement, packaged under the orthogonality reading.
- [`olsBeta_eq_of_normal_equations`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L67)
  (theorem, line 67) Рђћ uniqueness: any `b` satisfying the normal equations
  equals `olsBeta`.

The headline Lean theorem reads:

```lean
theorem normal_equations
    (X : Matrix n k РёЮ) (y : n Рєњ РёЮ) [Invertible (Xрхђ * X)] :
    Xрхђ *рхЦ residual X y = 0
```

### Reading the statement

In English: **given** a design matrix $X$, a response vector $y$, and a witness
that $X^\top X$ is invertible, the OLS residual is orthogonal to every column
of $X$.

| Lean | Math | What it gives us |
|---|---|---|
| `(X : Matrix n k РёЮ)` | $X \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times k}$ | **Given:** design matrix. The index types `n` (observations) and `k` (regressors) are arbitrary `Fintype`s, supplied by the section variables. |
| `(y : n Рєњ РёЮ)` | $y \in \mathbb{R}^n$ | **Given:** response vector. Lean models a real vector as a function `n Рєњ РёЮ` from the index type to reals. |
| `[Invertible (Xрхђ * X)]` | $X^\top X$ invertible | **Given (hypothesis):** the Gram matrix is invertible Рђћ Hansen's full-column-rank assumption. The square brackets mark this as an instance argument: it is supplied by typeclass resolution rather than as an explicit `have`. |
| `Xрхђ *рхЦ residual X y` | $X^\top \hat e$ | **Conclusion:** matrix-times-vector applied to the residual. `Matrix.transpose` is written `Xрхђ`; `*рхЦ` is `Matrix.mulVec`. |
| `= 0` | $= 0$ | The right-hand side is the zero vector in $\mathbb{R}^k$. |

(The usual ambient typeclasses `[Fintype n] [Fintype k] [DecidableEq k]` are
hoisted into the file's `variable` block, which is why they don't appear in
the signature.)

## Translation notes

- Hansen writes $X' \hat e = 0$ in matrix-of-real-numbers notation. Lean's
  `Xрхђ *рхЦ residual X y = 0` is the same statement under Mathlib's
  `Matrix.mulVec` / transpose conventions.
- Lean parameterizes by typeclasses `[Fintype n] [Fintype k] [DecidableEq k]`
  and an `[Invertible (Xрхђ * X)]` instance instead of a side condition. This is
  the same hypothesis as Hansen's "$X^\top X$ is invertible," packaged for
  elaboration.
- The pair `olsBeta` (using `РЁЪ`) and `olsBetaStar` (using `Matrix.nonsingInv`)
  coexist because Chapter 7's stochastic story needs the total version. They
  agree under invertibility
  ([`olsBetaStar_eq_olsBeta`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L31)
  at line 31).

## Proof sketch

Substitute the closed form $\hat\beta = (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y$ into
$X^\top (y - X\hat\beta)$:
$$X^\top y - X^\top X (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y = X^\top y - X^\top y = 0.$$
That's the whole proof Рђћ the normal equations are immediate from the
definition of $\hat\beta$.

Uniqueness (`olsBeta_eq_of_normal_equations`) goes the other direction: from
$X^\top(y - Xb) = 0$, multiply through by $(X^\top X)^{-1}$ to recover
$b = (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top y = \hat\beta$.

## Lean proof structure

The Lean proof of
[`normal_equations`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L46)
mirrors the textbook proof in three short steps:

- Unfold `residual`, `fitted`, and `olsBeta`, then split the `mulVec` over
  subtraction with `Matrix.mulVec_sub`.
- Use `Matrix.mulVec_mulVec` to commute the two `*рхЦ` applications into a
  single matrix product, then reassociate via `Matrix.mul_assoc` so that
  $X^\top X$ sits next to its inverse.
- Collapse $(X^\top X) \cdot РЁЪ(X^\top X)$ to $1$ using `mul_invOf_self`,
  leaving $X^\top y - X^\top y$, which `simp` reduces to $0$.

### Tactic-by-tactic walkthrough

The full Lean proof body:

```lean
theorem normal_equations
    (X : Matrix n k РёЮ) (y : n Рєњ РёЮ) [Invertible (Xрхђ * X)] :
    Xрхђ *рхЦ residual X y = 0 := by
  unfold residual fitted olsBeta
  rw [mulVec_sub]
  have hmul : Xрхђ *рхЦ (X *рхЦ (РЁЪ (Xрхђ * X) *рхЦ (Xрхђ *рхЦ y)))
            = (Xрхђ * (X * РЁЪ (Xрхђ * X))) *рхЦ (Xрхђ *рхЦ y) := by
    rw [Рєљ Matrix.mulVec_mulVec, Рєљ Matrix.mulVec_mulVec]
  calc
    Xрхђ *рхЦ y - Xрхђ *рхЦ (X *рхЦ (РЁЪ (Xрхђ * X) *рхЦ (Xрхђ *рхЦ y)))
        = Xрхђ *рхЦ y - (Xрхђ * (X * РЁЪ (Xрхђ * X))) *рхЦ (Xрхђ *рхЦ y) := by rw [hmul]
    _ = Xрхђ *рхЦ y - (((Xрхђ * X) * РЁЪ (Xрхђ * X)) *рхЦ (Xрхђ *рхЦ y)) := by rw [Matrix.mul_assoc]
    _ = Xрхђ *рхЦ y - (1 *рхЦ (Xрхђ *рхЦ y)) := by rw [mul_invOf_self]
    _ = 0 := by simp
```

Line by line:

- `unfold residual fitted olsBeta` Рђћ replace the three definitions by their
  bodies. The goal becomes
  $X^\top \!\bigl(y - X \cdot РЁЪ(X^\top X) \cdot X^\top y\bigr) = 0$.
- `rw [mulVec_sub]` Рђћ distribute the outer `Xрхђ *рхЦ` over the subtraction
  ($X^\top(u - v) = X^\top u - X^\top v$). The goal becomes
  $X^\top y - X^\top \!\bigl(X \cdot РЁЪ(X^\top X) \cdot X^\top y\bigr) = 0$.
- `have hmul := ...` Рђћ prove a helper equality stating that the nested
  matrix-vector applications $X^\top(X(РЁЪ(X^\top X)(X^\top y)))$ equal the
  single product $\bigl(X^\top \cdot X \cdot РЁЪ(X^\top X)\bigr) \cdot (X^\top y)$.
  The proof of `hmul` rewrites with `Matrix.mulVec_mulVec` (applied
  backwards twice) to fuse the matrix actions into one.
- **`calc` step 1** Рђћ apply `hmul` to rewrite the second term; the goal is
  now $X^\top y - \bigl(X^\top X \cdot РЁЪ(X^\top X)\bigr) \cdot X^\top y = 0$
  modulo association.
- **`calc` step 2** Рђћ `Matrix.mul_assoc` re-associates
  $X^\top \cdot (X \cdot РЁЪ(X^\top X))$ to $(X^\top X) \cdot РЁЪ(X^\top X)$,
  putting the Gram matrix next to its inverse.
- **`calc` step 3** Рђћ `mul_invOf_self` collapses $(X^\top X) \cdot РЁЪ(X^\top X)$
  to the identity matrix `1`. The expression simplifies to $X^\top y - 1 \cdot (X^\top y)$.
- **`calc` step 4** Рђћ `simp` clears `1 *рхЦ v = v`, then $X^\top y - X^\top y = 0$.

For uniqueness,
[`olsBeta_eq_of_normal_equations`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L67)
(line 67) takes the hypothesis $X^\top(y - Xb) = 0$, rewrites it as
$X^\top y = (X^\top X) b$, and multiplies both sides by $РЁЪ(X^\top X)$ to
identify $b$ with `olsBeta X y`.

## Downstream uses

`normal_equations` is one of the most reused lemmas in the algebra of OLS.
Direct consumers in this repo include:

- [`residual_sum_zero_of_one_mem_colspan`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean#L98)
  (Chapter3LeastSquaresAlgebra.lean:98) Рђћ when $\mathbf{1} \in \mathrm{col}(X)$,
  residuals sum to zero (Hansen equation 3.17).
- [`regressors_transpose_mul_annihilator`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L37)
  (Chapter3FWL.lean:37) Рђћ $X^\top M = 0$, used throughout FWL.
- [`fitted_dot_residual`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L217)
  (Chapter3Projections.lean:217) Рђћ fitted values are orthogonal to residuals.
- The FrischРђЊWaughРђЊLovell theorems of Hansen Theorem 3.5 consume this
  transitively through the annihilator identity.
